title: rosa tomentosa subvar. confusa
intro: Rosa tomentosa subvar. confusa, also known as the Himalayan rose or rose with wool, is a subspecies of Rosa tomentosa, a species of rose native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rosa tomentosa subvar. confusa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
